qa_pairs.question: How do I mark a publication as *not* enabled by Alliance computational resources when reviewing them one-by-one?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are going over publications one-by-one, you must click on a publication twice to mark it as not enabled by our computational resources. The first click indicates it *was* enabled, and the second click removes the check mark, indicating it *was not*.

qa_pairs.question: What happens the first time I click on a publication when individually reporting whether it was enabled by computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: The first time you click on a publication, a check mark will appear, indicating that the publication was enabled by our computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: What happens the second time I click on the same publication when individually reporting whether it was enabled by computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: The second time you click on the same publication, the check mark will be removed, indicating that the publication was not enabled by our computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: How can I quickly mark all new publications as enabled by Alliance computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: You can quickly mark all new publications as enabled by our resources by clicking the 'Select all new' button.

qa_pairs.question: How can I quickly indicate that none of the new publications were enabled by Alliance computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: You can quickly indicate that none of the new publications were enabled by our computational resources by clicking on the 'Clear all new' button.
